edibility: Excellent edible and medicinal mushroom highly valued in Asian cuisine and traditional medicine. Consumed fresh-cooked, as tea, or in capsule supplements for its potential nerve growth factor benefits and cognitive support.
taste: Delicate seafood-like flavor reminiscent of lobster or crab meat with a tender, almost delicate texture that absorbs flavors well when cooked.
search: Found late summer through fall on dead or dying hardwoods, particularly beech, oak, and maple trees at various heights. Identify by distinctive long, downward-hanging spine-like structures with no visible cap and pure white color when fresh. Other Hericium species are also edible and similarly distinctive.
